route.name: SF Peninsula Traverse
route.sourceNote: Exact public GPX from BIKEPACKING.com SF Peninsula Traverse route. Official route: 112 mi, 3-4 days, 75% unpaved, 16,372 ft ascent.
route.description: Official Bikepacking.com route trace through the Peninsula mountains, coastal redwoods, Bay Area Ridge Trail sections, Skeggs, Sanborn, and South Bay ridges. This is now exact GPX geometry rather than a planning sketch.

days.name: Northern trailhead to Coast / Half Moon Bay zone
Half Moon Bay zone to Skyline / Redwoods
Skyline / Redwoods to Castle Rock / Sanborn zone
South Bay ridges to Almaden finish

waypoints.name: Views of South SanJose and Mt Umhum

logistics: Castle Rock source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=538
Sanborn County Park source checked July 2, 2026: https://parks.santaclaracounty.gov/locations/sanborn-county-park
Midpen El Corte de Madera source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.openspace.org/preserves/el-corte-de-madera-creek
Subagent GPX/artifact check: public GPX parsed locally with 4 tracks and 7345 trackpoints; terrain map usable.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public bikepacking GPX; inspect detailed surface before relying on it.
route.bestSeason: Check local weather, closures, and surface conditions.

difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and Highway sections
difficultyProfile.technical: low to moderate unless variants add singletrack/fire-road roughness

resources.water: Water/resupply appears available from prior planning, but check current campground and town status.
resources.food: Confirm stores/restaurants and hours near daily endpoints.
resources.toiletsShowers: Confirm per campground; hiker-biker sites vary.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest bike shops before gate confirmation.
resources.longestGap: Not fully automated; reviewer should verify longest water/resupply gap.
resources.mosquitoes: Lower than Sierra lake routes, but still check season and camp reports.

tips: Use the terrain map to spot exposed climbs and paved connector risk.
Promote only after water, camp, and legal access checks are complete.
hazards: SF Peninsula Traverse cannot be gate-confirmed until the GPX is audited against Castle Rock/Midpen/Sanborn bike-legal trails and each overnight stop is reserved or otherwise verified.
SF Peninsula Traverse is not next-weekend gate-confirmed: it needs a shortened/Fri-Mon itinerary, a bike-legal trail audit, and confirmed camp/lodging.
Castle Rock and Midpen bike rules do not prove the imported GPX is legal; every trail segment must be audited before promotion.
Castle Rock bike legality is too narrow to assume the imported GPX is legal.

verification.notes: Terrain/topographic basemap is required for review; OSM-only screenshots are rough-sketch quality.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Do not promote beyond idea if terrain/topographic context is missing or route lines are hard to read.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass

No exact shortened GPX-backed variant was identified.
requiredFixes: Refresh live campground/water/closure checks before riding.
Verify every Castle Rock, Midpen, Sanborn, and county-park segment is bicycle-legal for the imported GPX.
Create and verify a July 10-13 camp/lodging sequence; Castle Rock trail camp alone does not solve the whole route.
Measure the longest water gap from the GPX and current official water points.
Build an exact Fri-Mon or shortened itinerary.
Audit every Castle Rock, Midpen, Sanborn, and county segment against bike-legal maps.
Confirm all overnight sites or lodging for the selected dates.
Build a shortened exportable GPX variant before re-vetting.
Audit every Castle Rock, Midpen, county, and coastal segment against official bike-legal maps.
Confirm every overnight with reservations or lodging; do not rely on first-come camping.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What is the current water/resupply gap?
Are camps/legal access current for the intended dates?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Official route is 112 miles, 3-4 days, and 16,372 feet; it is not a clean Sat-Sun route without a separately vetted shortened segment.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: No July 10-13 camp/lodging sequence is confirmed; Castle Rock trail camps require registration/reservation and do not solve the full route.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Route needs final Castle Rock/Midpen/Sanborn/county fire, weather, and closure refresh.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Castle Rock and Midpen bicycle access is limited to designated corridors; imported GPX requires segment-level legal audit.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Some official water exists, but Midpen preserves have no camping and route-wide water gaps remain unmeasured.

readinessEvidence.finding: Official Castle Rock page says bicycles are allowed on the Skyline Trail/Bay Area Ridge Trail located north of Highway 35.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Castle Rock page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page says trail camps require registration or reservations and trail camp status is open with no campfires.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Castle Rock page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official facilities list drinking water available, but the full route has multiple jurisdictions and unresolved gaps.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Castle Rock page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official preserve page lists bicycling on designated trails, no camping, and restrooms available.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Midpeninsula Regional Open Space District El Corte de Madera Creek Preserve page

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page lists trail status open and trail camp status open, with no campfires; departure-week fire/weather refresh still required.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Castle Rock page

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent found the public GPX parses cleanly, but official route length/duration and lack of camp sequence make this nonfunctional for a simple next-weekend ride.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Bay/NorCal subagent GPX/source check

readinessEvidence.finding: Castle Rock bicycle access is limited and Midpen allows bikes only on designated trails, so the GPX needs a segment-level bike-legal audit.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks and Midpen official pages

readinessEvidence.finding: No confirmed overnight sequence exists for July 10-13.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Bay/NorCal subagent official-source check

readinessEvidence.finding: Castle Rock trail/camp status is open with no campfires, but bicycles are allowed only on Skyline Trail/Bay Area Ridge Trail north of Highway 35.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Castle Rock

readinessEvidence.finding: Half Moon Bay State Beach shows NO availability for Fri 7/10/26, Sat 7/11/26, and Sun 7/12/26; this does not provide a July 10-13 camp sequence.
